title: Diagnostic baseline: unfamiliar, fragile, and secure knowledge

objectives.statement: Classify a knowledge node as unfamiliar, fragile, or provisionally secure from a varied low-stakes response record.
Justify a provisional classification with performance evidence, confidence calibration, and an explicit uncertainty or next probe.
Select a proportionate study route and adapt a diagnostic while preserving its low-stakes purpose and accessible response options.

scenes.title: A baseline is a routing tool
scenes.contentHtml:
A baseline is a routing tool

Two learners can produce the same total score for different reasons. One may not recognize a node's notation; another may recognize it and reproduce a familiar step but lose the relation when the representation changes. A single total hides the difference, so it cannot choose a proportionate next route by itself.

In this lesson, a diagnostic baseline (term-diagnostic-baseline) is a low-stakes sample of what is currently accessible. Its job is to answer a bounded routing question: should this one knowledge node receive initial instruction, targeted strengthening, or a brief verification before the learner advances? The baseline is evidence for a decision, not a grade or a description of the learner as a person.

You will learn to:

  1. classify a node as unfamiliar, fragile, or provisionally secure from a varied response record;
  2. justify the classification while preserving uncertainty and separating confidence from performance; and
  3. choose a next route and repair an inaccessible or familiarity-only diagnostic.

This lesson does not administer the full Premed entry diagnostic, establish delayed retention, diagnose a learning difference or health condition, award credit, or make an admissions decision. It teaches how to interpret a small fictional or self-generated evidence record without entering private educational or health data.

Route through the lesson

First retrieve the curriculum-map prerequisite without notes. Then construct the probe → evidence → state → route model, watch a complete worked example, classify new cases before feedback, and complete an instrument-redesign transfer task. Every task is untimed and retakeable with an equivalent case.

Accessibility and alternatives

All essential relationships appear in source-order prose and tables. No distinction depends on color, position, sound, motion, hover, dragging, handwriting, or speed. You may enter responses by keyboard, speech-to-text, dictation to a scribe, or another structured text route. If a table is awkward in your access mode, read each row as a sentence in column order: node, observed response, explanation, variation, confidence. The reasoning standard remains the same.

Source Note
claims claim-baseline-purpose, claim-pattern-over-snapshotsources source-premed-syllabus, source-content-standard, source-authoring-evidence

The course syllabus, learning-content standard, and evidence synthesis support a low-stakes, revisable diagnostic interpreted from more than a total, familiarity feeling, or isolated response. They do not establish a permanent learner label or prove that a short baseline measures retained mastery.

scenes.title: Retrieve the map before reading the evidence
scenes.contentHtml:
Retrieve the map before reading the evidence

Complete this check without returning to the earlier map lesson. The aim is to expose what is available now, not to reward speed.

Check
id check-map-prerequisite
  1. In one sentence, distinguish a knowledge node from a prerequisite link.
  2. Suppose node Gamma depends on node Beta, and node Beta depends on node Alpha. Gamma is currently inaccurate, but the record contains no current evidence about Beta or Alpha. Which prerequisite should you probe first, and what result would justify probing Alpha next?
  3. Draw or type the route in dependency order using names and arrows or an ordered list.

After committing a response, compare it with this routing criterion: a node is the bounded outcome being learned, whereas a prerequisite link says which earlier outcome supports access to it. Because the stem supplies no evidence about either dependency, it does not justify declaring Alpha insecure. Probe Beta first: Beta is the nearest prerequisite and testing it adds the least unsupported recovery work. If Beta is insecure, probe Alpha. Reopen Alpha only if Alpha is then shown insecure; if Alpha is secure while Beta remains insecure, reopen Beta. If Beta is secure, keep Alpha closed and re-probe Gamma because the record does not implicate the earlier chain.

If you could state the distinction and use the evidence to decide which node to probe, continue. If you could name the pieces but not use the direction, record topic-orientation-map-and-pathways as a fragile prerequisite recovery target. If the terms were not available at all, record that graph outcome as unfamiliar. Neither route is a penalty, and neither depends on a lesson pack being published.

In-scene recovery and fresh retry

Use this bounded restatement now: a node names one outcome. A prerequisite link points from an earlier supporting outcome to a later outcome. When the later outcome fails, work backward one link at a time, collect evidence, and reopen the earliest dependency that is actually shown insecure. Do not infer that every earlier node failed.

Now retry with a fresh chain: Zeta depends on Epsilon, and Epsilon depends on Delta. A current probe shows Delta secure. Epsilon produces a relevant but inconsistent response. Zeta is inaccurate. Which node should reopen first? Epsilon is the supported answer because it is the earliest dependency in this chain that the record actually shows insecure; Delta has current secure evidence. Cover this answer, explain the decision in your own words, and then re-attempt the Alpha–Beta–Gamma chain.

Do not replace the attempt with a familiarity judgment such as “I remember seeing this.” The response itself is the evidence produced by this probe (term-probe).

Source Note
claims claim-baseline-purpose, claim-retrieval-before-feedback, claim-map-dependency-routesources source-premed-syllabus, source-content-standard, source-authoring-evidence, source-premed-graph

The sources support graph-based prerequisite routing, evidence before reopening an insecure dependency, and an attempt before answer reveal. The chain probes, bounded restatement, and executable recovery route are original to this lesson.

scenes.title: From probes to a provisional state
scenes.contentHtml:
From probes to a provisional state

The model has four ordered parts:

  1. Probe: ask for one observable operation before feedback.
  2. Evidence: record what the response shows and what it does not show.
  3. Provisional state: compare the pattern with the operational state definitions.
  4. Route: choose the least support that still addresses the observed gap, then collect fresh evidence.

Skipping from a self-rating directly to a route leaves the central inference unsupported. The diagnostic needs observable response evidence because familiarity and confidence can disagree with performance.

Read the evidence across five dimensions

Dimension Question asked of the record What it can reveal
Access Was a relevant idea or procedure produced before feedback? Whether a usable response model is currently available
Accuracy Does the response satisfy the declared node? Whether the available response is correct under these conditions
Reasoning Can the learner explain the relation or decision? Whether the response is more than copied surface steps
Adaptation Does the same relation survive a bounded variation (term-bounded-variation)? Whether the learner can select and use the model beyond one familiar form
Calibration How does stated confidence compare with observed performance? Whether an additional confidence-repair flag is needed

These dimensions are not points to average. They answer different questions. For example, low confidence with accurate independent adaptation supports secure performance plus a calibration flag; it does not erase the performance. High confidence with an incorrect model supports repair, not a secure label.

Operational state definitions

Unfamiliar knowledge (term-unfamiliar-knowledge) means no usable response model appears yet. Evidence may be no attempt, an explicit lack of prior encounter, or an unsupported guess that cannot be explained. Nonresponse has alternative causes, so the label remains provisional and may require an accessible re-prompt.

Fragile knowledge (term-fragile-knowledge) means that some relevant knowledge is accessible, but it is incomplete, inconsistent, cue-dependent, weakly explained, or unable to survive a bounded variation. When some knowledge is present but a probe needed to justify secure has not been attempted, report provisionally fragile/unresolved and collect that probe. “Unresolved” qualifies the fragile routing decision; it is not a fourth knowledge state, and it does not mean the unattempted operation would fail.

Secure knowledge (term-secure-knowledge) requires a pattern: accurate independent responses, defensible reasoning, and successful use under bounded variation. “Secure” here is still provisional. It does not by itself mean mastered, retained after a delay, or transferable to every context.

Keep three course vocabularies separate

The three labels above are the assigned outcome's vocabulary for a single node-level diagnostic decision. The syllabus also uses two other vocabularies at different grain sizes. They inform this decision but are not synonyms for it.

Record Question it answers Bounded crosswalk to this lesson
Entry-station bands: Foundational, Developing, and capital-S Secure (syllabus section 3.2) Which on-ramp action follows a station result based on total accuracy and critical-misconception status? Foundational and Developing route to different amounts of on-ramp support, but either may contain unfamiliar and fragile nodes. A station Secure result permits beginning the linked core node. If the report supplies only the station percentage and critical-misconception flag, it supplies aggregate accuracy and safety evidence—not enough evidence to assign this lesson's state to an individual node. Collect node-level probes; when they show accurate access but omit explanation or bounded adaptation, record that node as fragile/unresolved rather than provisionally secure.
Longitudinal states: N Not yet encountered, A Acquiring, P Practising, M Mastered, R Retained, and T Transfer (syllabus section 7) What course status has accumulated across instruction, gates, spacing, and cross-domain use? Unfamiliar is closest to N when no interpretable evidence exists. Fragile commonly routes through A or P. Provisionally secure performance may contribute evidence toward M, but it does not award M without the full gate, R without delayed checks, or T without a cross-domain task. Keep the N/A/P/M/R/T names in the longitudinal record.

Thus, the word secure is overloaded in the course documents. Capital-S Secure station result names the section 3.2 placement band; provisionally secure node state names the richer response pattern taught here. Always record which one you mean.

Turn the state into a route

Provisional state Immediate route Fresh evidence to seek
Unfamiliar Begin with a bounded explanation and complete worked model A first explained completion problem, then an unaided near case
Fragile Repair the observed error with a contrasting case and faded prompts Two varied unaided responses, including the failed representation or decision
Secure Avoid redundant initial instruction; advance provisionally A delayed prompt and a different bounded application

This route preserves an important asymmetry. Insufficient evidence should not remove needed guidance from a novice. Strong evidence can reduce redundant scaffolding, but the later check remains because immediate success is not delayed retention.

Source Note
claims claim-three-state-model, claim-pattern-over-snapshot, claim-retrieval-before-feedback, claim-guidance-fades, claim-confidence-calibrationsources source-premed-syllabus, source-content-standard, source-authoring-evidence, source-premed-graph

The three-state definitions retain the assigned graph outcome's vocabulary. The crosswalk above relates it to, without collapsing it into, the syllabus's entry-station bands and longitudinal mastery states. Evidence on retrieval, varied performance, guidance fading, and confidence calibration supports the decision dimensions, but no source establishes these labels as a universal psychometric taxonomy.

scenes.title: Work a three-node baseline
scenes.contentHtml:
Work a three-node baseline

Worked Example
id worked-example-three-node-baseline

Task. Classify three fictional prerequisite nodes from one low-stakes record and choose the next route for each. The unknowns are the provisional state, the key uncertainty, and the next evidence-producing action.

Givens, assumptions, and boundary. Every prompt was untimed, attempted before feedback, and available as plain text. The prompts are assumed to target the same declared node within each row, so the representation change is a bounded variation rather than a new construct. No units or sign conventions apply to this classification task, and no personal record is involved.

Node Access and accuracy Reasoning Bounded variation Confidence
Alder No attempt on either probe; learner writes “I have not met this notation” None yet Not attempted Low
Birch Accurate on a familiar form after a cue; second response uses the same memorized steps incorrectly Names the steps but not the relation Fails when the same relation is presented as a table High
Copper Accurate independently on two forms Explains why the relation applies and checks the result Selects the same relation in a new bounded case Medium

Model selection. The task asks for current knowledge accessibility and routing, so use the five evidence dimensions and three operational states. Do not average the rows or let confidence decide the state.

Plan. For each row: establish whether a usable model is accessible; inspect accuracy and explanation; test whether the relation survives variation; compare confidence with the record; assign the narrowest defensible state; then choose a route that will produce new evidence.

Alder. No usable response model appears, and the learner explicitly reports no prior encounter. Classify Alder as unfamiliar, provisionally. Begin with a bounded explanation and complete worked model, then ask for an explained completion response. Before concluding that the node itself is unfamiliar, verify that notation and response access did not cause the blank.

Birch. Some procedure is accessible, so “unfamiliar” discards evidence. Yet the response depends on a cue, the explanation does not identify the relation, and the table variation fails. Classify Birch as fragile. Compare the familiar and table forms, model the shared relation, fade the prompts, and retry with a different table. High confidence adds a calibration-repair flag; it does not make the knowledge secure.

Copper. The record contains accurate independent responses, an explanation, a check, and bounded adaptation. Classify Copper as provisionally secure. Skip redundant initial instruction, advance to the reachable node, and schedule a delayed varied prompt. Medium confidence is consistent with, but does not prove, the performance state.

Independent check. Reverse-test each decision. Calling Alder fragile would require some usable relevant knowledge that the record does not show. Calling Birch secure would have to explain the failed variation and cue dependence. Calling Copper fragile would ignore the independent varied pattern. The labels therefore discriminate the three records without treating them as permanent.

Interpretation. The result is not “this learner is one-third unfamiliar, one-third fragile, and one-third secure.” It is three node-specific route decisions: initial instruction for Alder, targeted repair for Birch, and delayed verification after advancement for Copper.

Self-explanation prompt. Which observation most strongly separates Birch from Copper, and why is confidence not that observation?

Source Note
claims claim-three-state-model, claim-pattern-over-snapshot, claim-guidance-fades, claim-confidence-calibrationsources source-premed-syllabus, source-content-standard, source-authoring-evidence, source-premed-graph

The example applies the operational three-state model, multiple-evidence principle, and adaptive fading rule. All node names, observations, decisions, and routes in the example are original fictional material.

scenes.title: Classify before the explanation
scenes.contentHtml:
Classify before the explanation

Write one state and one evidence phrase for each case before reading below the divider.

Check
id check-three-state-retrieval
  1. Delta: produces no relevant response, reports no prior encounter, and can use the response format without difficulty.
  2. Elm: recalls a rule and succeeds with the familiar wording, but cannot explain the relation and selects a different rule after the representation changes.
  3. Fjord: answers accurately without cues across two representations, explains the decision, and succeeds in one bounded application, while reporting low confidence.

Feedback after the attempt

  1. Delta is provisionally unfamiliar because no usable response model appears. Next, provide initial instruction and seek a first explained response.
  2. Elm is fragile because relevant knowledge is accessible but cue-bound, weakly explained, and not adaptable. Next, compare representations and retry after faded support.
  3. Fjord shows provisionally secure performance with a calibration flag. The independent varied pattern supports secure knowledge; low confidence calls for a later calibration check rather than erasing that evidence.

If your labels differed, identify the dimension you used. A label based only on “some answer was correct” ignores explanation and adaptation. A label based only on confidence ignores the response record. Retry by covering the feedback and inventing one surface change that preserves each node's underlying operation.

Source Note
claims claim-three-state-model, claim-pattern-over-snapshot, claim-retrieval-before-feedback, claim-confidence-calibrationsources source-premed-syllabus, source-content-standard, source-authoring-evidence, source-premed-graph

The sources support retrieval before feedback and a varied evidence pattern rather than a one-response security judgment. These cases and their feedback are original.

scenes.title: Confidence is not the knowledge state
scenes.contentHtml:
Confidence is not the knowledge state

Misconception
id misconception-confidence-equals-security

Commit to a prediction. Learner Quartz is highly confident but repeatedly applies the wrong relation and cannot explain why it should fit. Learner Reed reports low confidence but independently answers accurately, explains the relation, and adapts it across a bounded representation change. If confidence were the knowledge state, which learner would be called secure?

The tempting model. “Feeling certain means the knowledge is secure; feeling uncertain means it is fragile.” This is plausible because confidence is immediate and easy to record.

What the model cannot explain. It would classify Quartz as secure despite an inaccurate, unexplained pattern, while downgrading Reed despite accurate independent reasoning and adaptation. The model therefore reverses the observed performance evidence in both cases.

Replacement model. Classify the knowledge state from access, accuracy, reasoning, and bounded adaptation. Record confidence separately and compare it with performance. Quartz has fragile or possibly unfamiliar model use, depending on what further explanation probes reveal, plus a high-confidence-error flag. Reed has provisionally secure performance plus a low-confidence calibration flag.

Same-case comparison. The confidence-only model predicts that Quartz should advance and Reed should restudy from the beginning. The evidence model predicts targeted misconception repair for Quartz and delayed verification rather than redundant initial instruction for Reed. Only the evidence model preserves what each learner actually demonstrated.

Recheck. A learner gives two accurate familiar responses with a good explanation, fails the bounded application, and reports medium confidence. Classify the node. The defensible answer is fragile, because failed adaptation limits the evidence; medium confidence neither repairs nor causes that gap.

Confidence calibration (term-confidence-calibration) matters because a learner needs to know when to seek another check. It remains a companion to performance, not a substitute for it.

Source Note
claims claim-three-state-model, claim-confidence-calibrationsources source-premed-syllabus, source-content-standard, source-premed-graph

The syllabus explicitly compares confidence with performance and routes high-confidence errors to remediation. The operational contrast and fictional learners are original; causal explanations for why an individual feels confident remain outside this lesson.

scenes.title: Fade the classification support
scenes.contentHtml:
Fade the classification support

Attempt each stage before reading its feedback. The support decreases while the target operation stays fixed: classify, justify, preserve uncertainty, and route.

Stage 1: completion problem

Check
id practice-completion

A learner produces the relevant relation, succeeds on the familiar form, gives only a memorized slogan as explanation, and has not yet tried a variation. Complete the reasoning:

“The state is provisionally ______ because ______. The missing evidence is ______. The next probe should ______.”

Feedback. Provisionally fragile/unresolved is the conservative routing label because some usable knowledge appears but the record lacks the explanation and variation needed to justify secure. This does not establish that adaptation would fail; no adaptation probe was attempted. A useful next probe changes the representation while preserving the relation and asks for a reason before feedback. If you chose secure, identify which observation established bounded adaptation; none was supplied.

Stage 2: supported comparison

Check
id practice-supported-comparison

Compare two records.

  • Juniper: three accurate familiar responses, no explanation or variation, high confidence.
  • Kite: two accurate independent responses, a defensible explanation, one successful bounded variation, low confidence.

Classify each node, then state the one observation that most strongly distinguishes the classifications.

Feedback. Juniper is provisionally fragile/unresolved pending an explanation-and-variation probe; repeated familiar accuracy alone does not establish adaptable knowledge, but the missing probe is not a failed probe. Kite supports provisionally secure performance with a calibration flag. The strongest distinction is not confidence but the independent explanation plus bounded adaptation.

Stage 3: independent mixed case

Check
id practice-independent-case

Morrow attempts four prompts. The first is blank. The second is correct after a terminology definition is supplied. The third is correct without a cue and includes a partial explanation. The fourth changes the representation and is incorrect. Confidence rises from low to high across the four prompts. Without using a numeric average:

  1. classify the node;
  2. cite two observations that support the classification;
  3. name one uncertainty; and
  4. prescribe a two-step retry route.

Feedback. Fragile best preserves the mixed record: relevant knowledge becomes accessible, but access is partly cue-dependent, reasoning is incomplete, and the representation change fails. A good uncertainty asks whether terminology caused the first blank or whether the model itself was unavailable. A proportionate route first contrasts the two representations with a worked explanation, then fades support into two fresh unaided cases separated in time. Rising confidence adds a calibration concern but does not change the state.

Retry rule

Do not replay the same wording until it feels familiar. Create or request an equivalent case with a new surface form, state the classification before feedback, and compare the new evidence with your previous error. If you repeatedly confuse unfamiliar and fragile, ask first whether any usable relevant model appeared. If you confuse fragile and secure, ask whether the model survived independent explanation and bounded variation.

Source Note
claims claim-three-state-model, claim-pattern-over-snapshot, claim-retrieval-before-feedback, claim-guidance-fades, claim-confidence-calibrationsources source-premed-syllabus, source-content-standard, source-authoring-evidence, source-premed-graph

The sources support attempts before feedback, varied evidence, fading assistance, and retries that require renewed reasoning. The cases, surface variations, and feedback are original.

scenes.title: Repair a familiarity-only survey
scenes.contentHtml:
Repair a familiarity-only survey

You inherit a placement form for the node “interpret measurement units.” It asks only:

How familiar are you with measurement units? Choose 1 to 5.

The form produces a confidence-like self-rating, but it does not reveal whether a learner can retrieve, explain, or adapt the relevant knowledge. Your task is to replace it with a short, low-stakes baseline that produces interpretable node-level evidence.

Design constraints

Your redesign must:

  1. elicit an unaided response before feedback;
  2. include an explanation decision and a bounded change of representation or context;
  3. record confidence without letting it determine the state;
  4. state unfamiliar, fragile, and secure decision rules plus one unresolved boundary;
  5. route each state to a proportionate next action;
  6. remain untimed and provide an equivalent keyboard/text response path; and
  7. avoid names, grades, disability details, health information, or other private data.

Do not solve this by adding more familiarity questions. Select probes that expose different evidence dimensions. For example, one prompt might ask for a direct interpretation, another for the relation that justifies it, and a third for use after the same information moves from prose to a table. The exact unit content is outside this lesson, so describe the operations without inventing a science answer key.

Submission frame

Use five short headings:

  • Probes
  • Evidence record
  • Provisional decision rules
  • Routes and uncertainty
  • Accessibility-equivalent operation

Then stress-test the redesign with two fictional patterns: high-confidence inaccuracy and low-confidence accurate adaptation. Explain why the state and the calibration flag differ in each pattern.

This is transfer because you must diagnose the weakness of an instrument, select the evidence model, and adapt it under privacy and access constraints. Merely renaming Alder, Birch, and Copper would not solve the design problem.

Source Note
claims claim-three-state-model, claim-pattern-over-snapshot, claim-confidence-calibrationsources source-premed-syllabus, source-content-standard, source-authoring-evidence, source-premed-graph

The evidence model and confidence boundary are supported within the recorded scope. The familiarity-only form, redesign constraints, and measurement-units context are original instructional material rather than a validated placement instrument.

scenes.title: Keep the label provisional and the route active
scenes.contentHtml:
Keep the label provisional and the route active

The whole model can be reconstructed as four verbs:

Probe → observe → classify → route.

Probe before feedback. Observe access, accuracy, reasoning, adaptation, and calibration separately. Classify one node provisionally from the pattern. Route the learner to initial instruction, targeted strengthening, or delayed verification, then let fresh evidence revise the label.

State Minimum defensible interpretation Route Do not infer
Unfamiliar No usable response model appears yet Initial explanation and complete model, then an explained attempt Inability, low potential, or a personal trait
Fragile Some knowledge is accessible but incomplete, cue-dependent, inconsistent, weakly explained, not adaptable, or unresolved because a secure-discriminating probe is missing Contrast failed and successful forms, or collect the missing probe; then fade support and retry with variation That nothing is known, that an unattempted operation failed, or that more rereading alone is enough
Secure A pattern of accurate independent reasoning survives bounded variation Advance provisionally and schedule delayed varied retrieval Permanent mastery, retention, or universal transfer

Confidence adds a calibration flag. It never replaces the state evidence.

Recovery route

If the model itself is unfamiliar, reread the operational definitions in scene-concept, cover the table, and reconstruct each state from memory. Then rework Alder, Birch, and Copper with the five evidence dimensions visible.

If your classifications remain fragile, make a two-column comparison: “some usable model” separates unfamiliar from fragile; “independent explanation plus bounded adaptation” separates fragile from secure. Classify two fresh cases without the columns, explain the distinction aloud or in text, and retry the assessment with an equivalent form.

If performance is secure, do not use immediate repetition as the only check. Schedule a fresh classification after a delay and carry the baseline record into topic-orientation-mastery-and-retrieval, where it becomes input to a mastery and retrieval plan. The Premed graph deliberately places that outcome downstream of this diagnostic-baseline outcome.

If access to a response format caused the gap, change the medium—such as table to ordered sentences, speech to keyboard, or pointer selection to named options—while preserving the classification operation. A format barrier is not evidence that the knowledge node is unfamiliar.

Source Note
claims claim-baseline-purpose, claim-three-state-model, claim-pattern-over-snapshot, claim-retrieval-before-feedback, claim-guidance-fades, claim-confidence-calibration, claim-curriculum-linksources source-premed-syllabus, source-content-standard, source-authoring-evidence, source-premed-graph

The state-to-route synthesis is grounded in course policy and evidence on guidance fading. The downstream graph relationship is a curriculum dependency, not proof that one fixed schedule is optimal for every learner.

scenes.title: Baseline mastery evidence
scenes.contentHtml:
Baseline mastery evidence

Complete the four items in assessment.json without notes on the first attempt. The assessment is untimed and retakeable. Commit each response before viewing its answer logic. A retry uses an equivalent response record or redesign context, not the identical answer sequence.

Item map

  1. item-classify-pattern — classify a cue-dependent pattern.
  2. item-separate-confidence — preserve performance evidence while recording a calibration flag.
  3. item-build-baseline — classify three nodes, justify uncertainty, and route each one without averaging them into a learner label.
  4. item-transfer-redesign — replace a familiarity-only survey with an accessible evidence-producing baseline.

The first two items test high-value boundaries. The third collects the complete classify–justify–route performance. The fourth tests whether you can select and adapt the model rather than reproduce the worked example.

Mastery and retry

Use the analytic rubrics in assessment.json. A mastery-ready response must classify from observed evidence, explain why competing states fit less well, preserve a meaningful uncertainty or next probe, and choose a proportionate route. The transfer response must also preserve low stakes, privacy, and an equivalent untimed keyboard/text operation.

The two selected-response items contribute to the 25-point aggregate only. item-classify-pattern earns 2 points only for option-pattern-fragile; every other choice earns 0. For item-separate-confidence, the performance criterion earns 2 points when option-confidence-secure is selected and neither distractor is selected, and the calibration criterion earns 1 point when option-confidence-flag is selected and option-confidence-ignore is not selected. Therefore the exact correct pair earns 3 points; the secure option alone earns 2; the calibration flag alone earns 1; secure plus either distractor earns 0; the calibration flag plus the fragile distractor earns 1; the calibration flag plus the ignore distractor earns 0; and any response containing only distractors earns 0.

For this draft gate, the provisional mastery condition is at least 20 of 25 points overall, at least 6 of 10 points on both item-build-baseline and item-transfer-redesign, and no criterion receiving zero in either of those two open-response rubrics. The selected-response items affect only the aggregate; a single miss there is not an automatic non-mastery decision. The open-response component minimums and no-zero rule prevent selected-response success from compensating for missing classification reasoning or transfer. These conditions remain candidate authoring decisions until independent review; meeting them is not evidence of delayed retention or real-world effectiveness.

If an item is incorrect, use its common-error feedback to identify the failing distinction:

  • no usable model versus some usable model;
  • familiar accuracy versus independent bounded adaptation;
  • performance evidence versus confidence; or
  • classification versus a permanent label.

Return to the matching scene, construct a fresh fictional record that crosses that boundary, and retry with the new record. Do not lower the standard or memorize an option ID.

Source Note
claims claim-three-state-model, claim-pattern-over-snapshot, claim-confidence-calibrationsources source-premed-syllabus, source-content-standard, source-authoring-evidence, source-premed-graph

The assessment uses the same operational distinctions taught and practised in the lesson. Answer logic remains provisional pending independent academic and learning-design review.

scenes.title: Evidence and rights trail
scenes.contentHtml:
Evidence and rights trail

The structured source registry is references.json, and the claim-to-scene evidence map is claims.json. Use those files to inspect exact locators, source scope, uncertainty, access dates, rights basis, and agent-access status.

The source IDs used in this pack are:

  • source-premed-syllabus
  • source-content-standard
  • source-authoring-evidence
  • source-premed-graph

The rights and author-provenance record is ATTRIBUTION.md. The design rationale and source dossier are in AUTHORING-BRIEF.md. No review or adjudication artifact belongs to this authoring run.

aiAssistance.disclosure: Material revision for version 0.1.3 was provided by Codex (OpenAI GPT-5), Codex CLI 0.144.6, isolated author run /root/revise_prem_lpp_002_final. The digest sha256:4374920bcb489ceffbb676e40ebf96a7ae6c75337bb35db12758f30ffe291739 covers the UTF-8 bytes of the exact discloseable revision assignment, one NUL separator, and the exact retained academic-review artifact bytes (artifact SHA-256 4b5357effeb96166e82b48d917f583332b27e68cf394631ab02b82b6733cca33); hidden provider instructions are excluded. This author run reconciled the course vocabularies, made assessment scoring deterministic, corrected objective and confidence metadata, and restored all author-controlled review states to pending. Historical review artifacts remain unmodified and stale for quorum after this content change. Accessibility findings about the shared production reader were not changed in this pack and are handled separately. Earlier author-run scopes and digests remain in ATTRIBUTION.md. Accountable principal: github:VSBDev.

items.prompt: A learner answers a familiar ratio prompt correctly after seeing a similar example, gives no explanation beyond 'I copied the steps,' and selects the wrong operation when the same ratio is shown in a table. Which provisional state is best supported?

options.text: No evidence at all is available

answer.summary: The evidence supports a fragile classification.
answer.reasoning: Some relevant procedure is accessible, so the node is not wholly unfamiliar. However, the response is cue-dependent, lacks a defensible explanation, and does not survive a bounded representation change. The next step is a contrasting worked example followed by an unaided table-based retry.
commonErrors: Calling the node secure because one answer was correct ignores cue dependence and failed adaptation.
Calling the node unfamiliar ignores the usable but incomplete procedure already present.

items.prompt: Across three untimed probes, a learner independently answers accurately, explains the governing relation, and adapts it to a new representation, but reports low confidence. Select the two statements that best preserve the evidence.

options.text: Downgrade the knowledge state to fragile solely because confidence is low.
Add a confidence-calibration flag and recheck after delay.
Discard the performance record and keep only the self-rating.

answer.summary: Keep performance and confidence as related but distinct evidence.
answer.reasoning: The varied, accurate, independent performance supports a provisional secure classification. Low confidence does not erase that record; it identifies a calibration issue worth revisiting after a delay. The classification remains provisional because short-term performance is not proof of retention.
acceptableResponses: Secure performance plus a calibration flag
commonErrors: Treating low confidence as direct proof of fragile knowledge confuses self-rating with observed performance.
Ignoring confidence loses useful evidence about calibration even though confidence does not determine the state.

items.prompt: Classify each fictional node, justify the classification from observations, name one uncertainty or next probe, and choose the next study action. Do not average the three nodes into one learner label.
items.stimulus: Node Cedar: no attempted response on either prompt; learner writes that the notation has not been encountered. Node Flint: accurate on two familiar prompts, explanation names the relevant relation, but a bounded representation change produces an inconsistent strategy. Node Harbor: accurate and independently explained across two forms and one bounded application; medium confidence throughout.

answer.correct: Cedar is unfamiliar, Flint is fragile, and Harbor is provisionally secure; each needs an evidence-linked route and an uncertainty or next probe.
answer.summary: Classify nodes separately and make the route follow the evidence.
answer.reasoning: Cedar has no usable response model and should begin with a bounded explanation or worked model before retry. Flint has accessible but non-adaptable knowledge and needs a contrasting case plus faded varied practice. Harbor shows a pattern of accurate independent reasoning under bounded variation and can advance while scheduling delayed verification. A good answer also preserves uncertainty: nonresponse can have access causes, Flint needs another variation after repair, and Harbor still lacks delayed retention evidence.
acceptableResponses: Equivalent labels and routes supported by the stated observations, with explicit uncertainty and no whole-person label.
commonErrors: Calling Flint secure because most familiar responses were correct ignores the failed bounded variation.
Treating Cedar's nonresponse as inability rather than provisional unfamiliar evidence overstates what the diagnostic establishes.
Calling Harbor permanently mastered ignores the absence of delayed evidence.

items.prompt: A placement form for interpreting measurement units asks only, 'How familiar are you with units?' on a five-point scale. Redesign it as a short low-stakes baseline that can distinguish unfamiliar, fragile, and secure knowledge. Specify the probes, evidence rule, routes, uncertainty, and an equivalent keyboard/text response path.

answer.correct: A rubric-scored redesign that elicits unaided access, explanation, and bounded application; treats confidence as supplementary; applies provisional node-level rules; routes each state proportionately; preserves uncertainty; and provides an equivalent untimed keyboard/text path.
answer.summary: Replace familiarity alone with observable performance while preserving accessibility and low stakes.
answer.reasoning: Self-rating alone cannot show whether knowledge is available, accurate, explainable, or adaptable. A defensible redesign samples those operations before feedback, compares confidence with performance, and makes labels provisional. Unfamiliar evidence routes to initial instruction, fragile evidence to targeted repair and varied retry, and secure evidence to delayed verification and the next node. The response mode may change, but the reasoning operation must remain equivalent.
acceptableResponses: Any concise redesign that preserves the same evidence dimensions, provisional state logic, proportionate routes, and accessible untimed operation.
commonErrors: Adding more self-rating questions without eliciting performance does not repair the instrument.
Using one multiple-choice fact as proof of secure knowledge does not test explanation or adaptation.
Requiring drag, color, speed, or spoken response without an equivalent route changes access rather than measuring the declared node.

criteria.description: Awards 2 points only for selecting option-pattern-fragile; any other selection receives 0 points.
Awards 2 points when option-confidence-secure is selected and neither option-confidence-fragile nor option-confidence-ignore is selected; otherwise awards 0 points.
Awards 1 point when option-confidence-flag is selected and option-confidence-ignore is not selected; otherwise awards 0 points.
Classifies Cedar, Flint, and Harbor separately using the operational three-state model.
Justifies each classification with specific access, accuracy, explanation, independence, or adaptation observations.
States a meaningful uncertainty or discriminating next probe without presenting the label as permanent.
Chooses a proportionate initial-instruction, targeted-repair, or delayed-verification route for each node.
Designs aligned probes for unaided access, explanation, and bounded application before feedback.
States provisional unfamiliar, fragile, and secure rules and treats confidence as supplementary evidence.
Links each classification to a proportionate route and states an important uncertainty.
Provides an untimed keyboard/text route that preserves the same reasoning operation without private data.

sources.title: Premed: Foundations for Medical Study
sources.version: Curriculum version 1.0 candidate at repository commit 76882799c7614eeb9307687bceab33896a79bb13

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Only curriculum facts and procedural relationships were used; all lesson prose, examples, tables, and assessment cases are independently authored.

agentAccess.notes: Public repository-owned source. Project instructions explicitly direct lesson agents to consult the syllabus, and no access control or agent-ingestion restriction was found for this use.

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Only instructional design requirements and evidence conclusions were used; no wording, source example, question, or organization was copied or adapted.

agentAccess.notes: Public repository-owned source. The author skill requires agents to read it, and no access control or agent-ingestion restriction was found for this use.

sources.title: Evidence synthesis: writing university-grade learning content
sources.version: Rapid evidence synthesis at repository commit 76882799c7614eeb9307687bceab33896a79bb13
sources.locator: Sections 4, 6 through 9, and 12; Method and limitations

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Only synthesis conclusions and stated limitations were used; lesson structure, cases, explanations, and feedback were created independently.

agentAccess.notes: Public repository-owned research source. Project authoring instructions direct agent use of this file, and no access control or agent-ingestion restriction was found.

sources.title: EmbeddedKnowledge Premed knowledge graph
sources.locator: Nodes topic-orientation-map-and-pathways, topic-orientation-diagnostic-baseline, and topic-orientation-mastery-and-retrieval; their prerequisite links

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Only the assigned node identities and individual prerequisite relationships were consulted; no database extraction, copied arrangement, or redistributed dataset is included.

agentAccess.notes: Public repository-owned curriculum data exposed as an agent discovery surface; no access control or agent-ingestion restriction was found for this bounded factual lookup.

claims.statement: A low-stakes prerequisite diagnostic samples current performance to choose a study route; it is not a grade, a personal verdict, or proof of lasting mastery.
claims.scope: EmbeddedKnowledge Premed entry and lesson-level prerequisite routing
claims.uncertainty: A short diagnostic provides provisional evidence and does not establish retained mastery or validate the diagnostic as a psychometric instrument.

claims.statement: For this course, unfamiliar, fragile, and secure are operational node states distinguished by the availability, accuracy, independence, and bounded adaptability of a learner's response evidence; fragile may be qualified as unresolved when some knowledge is present but evidence needed to justify secure has not yet been elicited.
claims.scope: Operational routing model for one Premed knowledge node at a time
claims.uncertainty: The three labels are the assigned graph outcome's node-level diagnostic vocabulary. They are explicitly cross-walked, but not equated, with the syllabus's entry-station bands and longitudinal mastery states; they are not presented as a universally validated psychological taxonomy.

claims.statement: A defensible secure classification requires a pattern of accurate independent reasoning under bounded variation rather than one answer, one feeling of familiarity, or one confidence rating.
claims.scope: Low-stakes classification for foundation-level conceptual or procedural prerequisite nodes
claims.uncertainty: The necessary number and form of probes depend on the node; this lesson teaches a conservative decision model, not a fixed universal test length.

claims.statement: Attempting retrieval before viewing an answer yields evidence about currently accessible knowledge that rereading or immediate answer reveal cannot provide by itself.
claims.scope: Low-stakes learning diagnostics and retrieval prompts with feedback
claims.uncertainty: Retrieval evidence depends on prompt alignment, conditions, feedback, and prior knowledge; it does not by itself establish broad competence.

claims.statement: When baseline evidence is weak, initial guidance and worked reasoning should be available; as independent performance strengthens, support can fade toward varied and transfer tasks.
claims.scope: Guidance decisions for novice university-preparatory learners
claims.uncertainty: The amount and pace of fading must respond to the learner and task; the sources do not prescribe one fixed sequence length.

claims.statement: Confidence must be compared with performance evidence: high confidence can accompany an error, and low confidence can accompany an accurate independent response.
claims.scope: EmbeddedKnowledge Premed confidence-calibration and remediation decisions
claims.uncertainty: Confidence is useful contextual evidence but does not reveal the cause of an error without further probing.

claims.statement: A prerequisite link identifies an earlier outcome that supports a later node, so diagnostic recovery should probe along the chain and reopen the earliest dependency actually shown insecure that can account for the current gap rather than assume an untested predecessor is insecure.
claims.scope: Premed graph navigation and mastery recovery
claims.uncertainty: A prerequisite edge represents the curriculum's declared dependency; an individual learner may still need an accessible re-prompt or a different route before the source of an error is known.

claims.statement: The Premed graph places the diagnostic-baseline outcome after curriculum-map orientation and before the mastery-and-retrieval outcome.
claims.scope: Premed knowledge graph version recorded in the source registry
claims.uncertainty: This is a curriculum dependency, not empirical evidence that one fixed sequence is optimal for every learner.

terms.definition: A low-stakes sample of current performance used to choose an appropriate next learning route, not to assign a permanent label or credential.
A node state in which the diagnostic produces no usable response model yet, such as no attempt, an explicit lack of prior encounter, or an unsupported guess that cannot be explained.

A node state in which some relevant knowledge is accessible but performance is incomplete, inconsistent, dependent on a cue or copied procedure, weakly explained, unable to survive bounded variation, or still lacks a discriminating probe needed to justify secure. In the last case, report fragile/unresolved and collect the missing evidence rather than infer that an unattempted operation would fail.

This lesson's provisional node state, supported by a pattern of accurate independent responses, defensible reasoning, and successful use under bounded variation. It is distinct from the syllabus's capitalized Secure entry-station band and is not by itself proof of Mastered, Retained, or Transfer status.
A change in context, representation, or surface features that still tests the same declared knowledge node rather than silently introducing a different construct.
The agreement between a learner's confidence judgment and the quality of the performance evidence produced under the declared conditions.
One low-stakes prompt chosen to elicit a particular kind of evidence, such as unaided access, explanation, representation change, or bounded application.

attributionHtml:
Attribution and provenance

Original lesson contribution

PREM-LPP-002, version 0.1.3, was prepared for EmbeddedKnowledge by the accountable principal github:VSBDev and is offered under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International license.

Suggested attribution:

EmbeddedKnowledge, “PREM-LPP-002 — Diagnostic baseline: unfamiliar, fragile, and secure knowledge,” version 0.1.3, contributed by VSBDev, licensed CC BY 4.0.

All lesson prose, operational tables, fictional records, practice tasks, answer logic, and assessment cases were created for this pack. No third-party expression, quotation, dataset, image, audio, video, or other media is incorporated or redistributed. The factual and procedural sources listed in references.json were used only to support independently synthesized content.

Material agent assistance

  • Accountable principal: github:VSBDev
  • Agent system: Codex
  • Provider: OpenAI
  • Model: GPT-5
  • Version: Codex CLI 0.144.6
  • Isolated author run ID: /root/revise_prem_lpp_002_final
  • Current revision instructions-and-evidence digest: sha256:4374920bcb489ceffbb676e40ebf96a7ae6c75337bb35db12758f30ffe291739
  • Prior governance-promotion instructions digest: sha256:1eb8c7285a2443c24722df599066a9069ca328d802a26f8ea61d9fe89efc81bf
  • Revision-cycle-2 instructions digest: sha256:dd8929b7894723a4bcce56f595b742029b772ed7be446888658be2efa0c11f25
  • Initial authoring instructions digest: sha256:3dab8b1fbc59bc36d4854e83de298a29837bdcdeddb33c6af09d5f61acae0c6f

The current digest covers the UTF-8 bytes of the exact discloseable revision assignment, one NUL separator, and the exact retained academic-claude-final-v2.json bytes (artifact SHA-256 4b5357effeb96166e82b48d917f583332b27e68cf394631ab02b82b6733cca33). The prior governance-promotion, revision-cycle-2, and initial digests retain their previously disclosed scopes. All exclude hidden provider instructions that cannot be exported. This isolated run performed only the author role; using the academic finding record as revision input did not turn the run into a review, approval, or adjudication.

Promotion evidence and boundary

The promotion input was three exact approving artifact files for lesson version 0.1.1, all targeting candidate 82ef7e209fc3d16273ac7bb1279905bdcde52fcc. Their byte-level SHA-256 records were:

  • academic: c7ec86b02c0aebc0016d1f756709ae7af33399ad242285a27984ed9fc83d968b
  • accessibility and rights: 4a2cf2800c115bee88ea756c83ce44963a428018156794a54ab0590f4073a22b
  • learning design: c8cc76a3551e0092f76f1c510bf58ab6ac7b30da17460f64875a85c22f32d019
  • exact-byte concatenation in the filename order academic-claude.json, accessibility-rights-codex.json, learning-design-agy.json: eba310262cdcfe842e89d2150bf1c7a3dae14c7d2a915d2dcb0276a358df46d6

Those artifacts disclosed approvals from the academic, learning-design, and accessibility-and-rights roles across three providers and three run IDs. Their provenance is disclosed and attested, not independently verified. They support resolving retained claim review states and setting bounded lesson source confidence to medium-high: the record is cross-role and approving, while its stated limitations include no representative learner testing, incomplete non-web export paths, accessibility-integration observations, and bounded evidence verification.

This 0.1.2 promotion changes workflow metadata only; it preserves the reviewed lesson expression and claim substance. Because any version change creates a new exact candidate, the 0.1.1 approvals do not establish quorum or merge readiness for 0.1.2. Fresh reviews against one frozen 0.1.2 candidate and a separate fresh adjudication remain required before any later status transition.

Version 0.1.3 revision evidence and boundary

The exact final-cycle artifacts for candidate 2c2d339d91de3c30d924af60d4c4a378ec19f201 are retained without modification alongside the earlier cycle-2 artifacts:

  • learning design, approving: learning-design-agy-final-v2.json, SHA-256 5824193fbad439d03f270a519e73e9abbe565e18c39224217295a241e1f788dc
  • academic, requesting changes: academic-claude-final-v2.json, SHA-256 4b5357effeb96166e82b48d917f583332b27e68cf394631ab02b82b6733cca33
  • accessibility and rights, requesting changes: accessibility-rights-codex-final-v2.json, SHA-256 0a6647cd6e47ec2220e0ed0f742e2d935e7e22b68c809ff508faf9b81d960f6e

Version 0.1.3 responds to the academic artifact by adding an explicit crosswalk among the outcome's node-level three-state vocabulary, the section 3.2 entry-station bands, and the section 7 longitudinal N/A/P/M/R/T states; restoring every claim and lesson-level source confidence to pending review; making selected-response scoring and the open-response no-zero gate deterministic; lowering the confidence-calibration claim to medium-high; and aligning the classification objective with the taught phrase “provisionally secure.” The accessibility artifact's two production-reader findings concern shared rendering of canonical assessment data. They are being handled in the dedicated reader-infrastructure change and were not altered or claimed resolved by this lesson-author run.

All six retained review artifacts target earlier candidate commits and are historical traceability records only. Because version 0.1.3 changes teaching and assessment content, none counts toward its quorum. Fresh role-isolated reviews must target the single frozen 0.1.3 candidate before separate adjudication.

Revision history

  • 0.1.1: replaced a recovery route that named an unavailable lesson pack with an executable in-scene route tied to the declared prerequisite outcome; changed prerequisite-chain diagnosis so only evidence-confirmed insecurity triggers reopening; made fragile/unresolved handling consistent when a discriminating probe is missing; and completed the synthesis claim mapping. The same disclosed author run made these changes from academic-review feedback without embedding or altering the review artifact.
  • 0.1.2: metadata-only governance promotion from the exact 0.1.1 approval record; set retained claims to reviewed, resolved source confidence to medium-high, retained draft status, and recorded that fresh exact-candidate quorum and adjudication are still required.
  • 0.1.3: reconciled the three node-level outcome states with the syllabus's entry-station bands and longitudinal mastery states; restored author-controlled review states to pending; made the 25-point mastery gate deterministically scorable; lowered the confidence-calibration claim confidence; and made “provisionally secure” consistent in objective metadata. Shared production-reader findings remain outside this authoring change.

Third-party assets and modifications

There are no third-party assets in this pack and therefore no asset modifications to report.
